open_hooks_to_carry: Keep dawn after battle physically specific: smoke, bodies, fatigue, shattered stone, administrative confusion kept offstage.
Let Martha and Prue respond differently but without absolving Owen.
prev_tail_excerpt:
...tone, closer to laughter this time. Dawn was not in the chamber, but some paler quality had entered the cracks around the hatch, a less absolute dark. Smoke threaded down after it.

Prue lifted the latch.

"Come up when you can stand," she said. "Or don't. But if you stay below, don't start telling yourself nobody gave you a human option."

Then she went into the passage, bending automatically at the low turn, lamp cupped against the draught. The light moved along the narrow stone for a moment, caught on the repaired wire at the hinge of her spectacles, and vanished.

Owen stayed where he was.

Above him, somewhere in the enormous body of the school, people were finding each other alive or not alive. They were calling names down corridors. They were setting benches straight, covering faces, laughing too hard because they had not died, vomiting from shock behind tapestries, running messages toward the Great Hall, becoming heroes in the version of the night the rest of the world would someday understand. The castle held all of that in its upper rooms.

Below, the anchor stone cooled under his ruined hand. The seam did not glow. The wider dark, for now, had been taught to keep its distance.

After a while he shut the Grinlow Leaves, wrapped them back in the tea towel, and set them into his satchel without looking at them.

He sat in the chalk wreckage until the last tremors left the floor and the sounds above resolved from battle into aftermath, and what remained in the chamber with him was the exact shape of the thing Prue had witnessed: help unused, made useless by decision.
state_deltas_required: Hogwarts can finally see the end of one war, while Owen alone understands that the deeper crisis has only been delayed.
Owen survives into a secret custodial vocation at the cost of ordinary belonging, forgiveness, and heroic legibility.
